topic: multiplying fractions by whole numbers, word problem reasoning
latex:
Magan is making pizzas for a pizza party. Each pizza requires $\frac{15}{16}$ pound of cheese. How much cheese does he need to make $6$ pizzas? Complete the sentence below.

To solve, we'll need to $\begin{pmatrix} \text{multiply } 15/16 \text{ by } 6 \\ \text{divide } 6 \text{ by } 15/16 \end{pmatrix}$, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL pounds of cheese} \end{pmatrix}$, but we don't know the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL pounds of cheese} \end{pmatrix}$.
answer_latex: \text{multiply } 15/16 \text{ by } 6; \text{ NUMBER of groups}; \text{ TOTAL pounds of cheese}
